arts.ttl: 'We will be out of it' - Christian Eriksen makes bold Manchester United claim after Newcastle loss
arts.sum: Christian Eriksen stepped up for post-match media duties following Man United's 2-0 defeat to Newcastle United on Monday night.

arts.ttl: Manchester United coach Ruben Amorim gives grim January transfer window update
arts.sum: Fourteenth in the Premier League and with ten defeats already this season, even the January transfer window may not offer Man United deliverance.

arts.ttl: 'Doesn't make sense' - Ruben Amorim won't change Manchester United formation despite relegation threat
arts.sum: Man Utd are in a relegation battle and Ruben Amorim is blaming himself, but he is adamant he won't change his formation.

arts.ttl: Amorim on Man Utd being in a relegation battle
arts.sum: There wasn't a lot of optimism in Gary Neville's post-match comments, but he added that the only positive was Amorim getting a better look at the players he's inherited so far. The Portuguese head coach also added that it is "a bit embarrassing" to be talking about relegation when asked about it in his post-match press conference, but acknowledged that the blame should be put on him.

arts.ttl: Man Utd given Dani Olmo boost as unwritten transfer agreement reached between rivals
arts.sum: Dani Olmo looks set to become a free agent in the January transfer window as Barcelona struggle to register the Spanish international, having paid £51m to sign him in the summer

arts.ttl: Every word from Ruben Amorim press conference after Man Utd 0 Newcastle 2 on 30 December 2024
arts.sum: Of course, the players are not… I didn’t choose the players specifically for that position, but that I already knew, but I have to sell my idea, if I’m going to change all the time, it’s going to be even worse. So I understand what you’re saying but if I know, for sure, that changing everything is going to get us near to win games, I will do it."

arts.ttl: Manchester United face ‘really clear’ threat of relegation, admits Amorim
arts.sum: United seven points off drop zone after loss to Newcastle Coach says club will have to sell to buy in January window Ruben Amorim admitted Manchester United need a “shock” and are being dragged into a relegation fight after their 2-0 defeat by Newcastle at Old Trafford on Monday night. It leaves United in 14th place on 22 points at the halfway stage of the Premier League season, seven above the drop zone. The defeat came after first-half goals from Alexander Isak and Joelinton and is United’s fourth consecutive loss in all competitions and the first time three consecutive home league matches have been lost since 1978-79. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Gary Neville says Man United have 'deteriorated' under Ruben Amorim and are the WORST Red Devils side of the last decade… with the club now on brink of being 'dragged' into relegation scrap
arts.sum: A miserable 2-0 Monday night defeat at home by Newcastle leaves the Red Devils 14th in the Premier League and just seven points above 18th-placed Ipswich Town.

arts.ttl: Man Utd relegation 'a possibility' says Amorim
arts.sum: It is 50 years since Manchester United were relegated, but boss Ruben Amorim says it is "a possibility" that history could repeat itself.

arts.ttl: Isak hailed as 'best striker in the Premier League right now'
arts.sum: Alexander Isak has been labelled as the "best striker in the Premier League right now" after the Newcastle United forward delivered a sensational display in a 2-0 win against Manchester United at Old Trafford. That is the view of former Liverpool defender Jamie Carragher, who lauded the Swede after he scored for the sixth straight Premier League match and took his tally in December to eight goals, more than any other player.
